title: Wild and Wacky World of Wigs
titleZh: 狂野又古怪的假发世界

q.en: According to the book, where were the oldest-known wigs found?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，已知最古老的假发是在哪里发现的？

options.en: In Egypt, dating back to 1400 BC
options.zh: 在埃及，可追溯到公元前1400年

options.en: In ancient Rome according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 在古罗马（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says the oldest-known wigs date back to 1400 BC in Egypt.
explain.zh: 书中说已知最古老的假发可追溯到公元前1400年的埃及。

q.en: The book defines 'full wigs,' 'toupees,' and 'extensions.' What is the author doing here?
q.zh: 书中定义了'全顶假发'、'局部假发'和'接发'。作者在这里做了什么？

options.en: Explaining the different types of wigs
Listing countries according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 列出国家（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The author gives clear definitions so readers understand the vocabulary of wigs.
explain.zh: 作者给出清晰定义，帮助读者理解假发相关的词汇。

q.en: According to the book, why did some Roman emperors wear wigs?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，一些罗马皇帝为什么戴假发？

options.en: As disguises so they could learn what people really thought
options.zh: 作为伪装，以便了解人们的真实想法

options.en: Because they liked red hair according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为他们喜欢红发（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says some emperors wore wigs as disguises to hear what everyday people thought of them.
explain.zh: 书中说一些皇帝戴假发作伪装，以打听百姓对他们的真实看法。

q.en: How did Julius Caesar cover his baldness?
q.zh: 尤利乌斯·恺撒是如何遮盖秃顶的？

options.en: With a long wig according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 用一长顶假发（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: After failing remedies, Caesar found a laurel wreath easier and wore it as a cover-up.
explain.zh: 在偏方失效后，恺撒觉得戴桂冠更方便，用它来遮掩秃顶。

q.en: Why does the author call the book 'Wild and Wacky World of Wigs'?
q.zh: 作者为什么把这本书叫做'狂野又古怪的假发世界'？

options.en: To warn readers not to wear wigs according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了警告读者不要戴假发（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To show the strange and fun history of wigs
options.zh: 为了展现假发奇特而有趣的历史

explain.en: The title signals a playful look at surprising wig facts and fashions.
explain.zh: 这个标题暗示将以轻松的方式呈现令人惊讶的假发事实与时尚。

q.en: In 18th-century France, why did women carry a long stick with a claw on the end?
q.zh: 在18世纪的法国，女性为什么带一根末端带爪的长棍？

options.en: To measure three-foot hairstyles according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了丈量三英尺高的发型（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To scratch inside their hairstyles without ruining them, since pests nested there
options.zh: 为了在不弄乱发型的前提下挠头皮，因为头发里会生虫筑巢

explain.en: Lard used to set the styles spoiled, so rats and roaches nested; the claw let them scratch.
explain.zh: 固定发型用的猪油会变质，老鼠蟑螂在其中筑巢；带爪长棍用来挠痒。

q.en: Which professionals in the United Kingdom still wear wigs today?
q.zh: 今天英国哪些职业人士仍戴假发？

options.en: Athletes according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 运动员（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: To this day, judges and lawyers in the UK court system wear wigs as part of their uniforms.
explain.zh: 至今，英国司法系统的法官和律师仍把假发作为制服的一部分。

q.en: The 'Bigwig' sidebar says the word first appeared in a 1703 journal. What does 'bigwig' mean?
q.zh: '大人物（bigwig）'侧栏说该词最早出现于1703年的期刊。'bigwig' 是什么意思？

options.en: A type of wig glue according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一种假发胶（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: It comes from the richest, most powerful people wearing the biggest, fanciest wigs.
explain.zh: 它源于最富有、最有权势的人戴最大最华丽的假发。

q.en: Besides fashion, what is one reason people wear wigs today, according to the book?
q.zh: 除了时尚，根据书中内容，今天人们戴假发的一个原因是什么？

options.en: To boost self-esteem or for medical hair loss
options.zh: 为了增强自信，或因疾病脱发

options.en: To speak French according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了说法语（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book notes people wear wigs for self-esteem and that groups like Locks of Love make them for children who lost hair.
explain.zh: 书中说人们戴假发是为了自信，而且像 Locks of Love 这样的组织会为脱发的孩子制作假发。

q.en: How did King Louis XIII of France change wig fashion?
q.zh: 法国国王路易十三如何改变了假发时尚？

options.en: He made wigs from straw only according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他只用稻草做假发（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He brought wigs back into style when he began losing his hair
options.zh: 他在开始脱发时让假发重新流行

explain.en: At 23 he began losing hair and wore wigs; noblemen copied him, reviving the fashion.
explain.zh: 他23岁开始脱发并戴假发；贵族们效仿，使假发时尚复兴。

prompt.en: Wigs have been worn for fashion, status, and necessity for thousands of years. If you could design a wild wig, what would it look like and what would it say about you?
prompt.zh: 几千年来，人们戴假发是为了时尚、身份或实际需要。如果你能设计一顶狂野的假发，它会是什么样子？它能代表你什么？

en: Think about color, shape, and any fun additions like objects in the hair.
Connect the style to your personality or a message you want to share.
zh: 想想颜色、形状，以及在头发里加点有趣的小物件。
把样式和你的个性或想表达的信息联系起来。
